page: Microsoft Azure E64bs_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EBSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX-512, AES, and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on external block storage, and is optimized for Premium Storage performance. It provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high-density memory configuration and specialized block storage capabilities, making it a specialized resource for memory-heavy workloads without the overhead of local NVMe drives.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with the passmark CPU score and stress-ng multi-core performance ranking in the top 25%. Uncached memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 300.1 GB/sec for read operations and 135.26 GB/sec for write operations. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50%. This performance profile translates to strong database operations and high Redis SET throughput, though large-scale LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, with the 70B model achieving only 2.76 tokens per second.

Given the high memory capacity, strong multi-threaded performance, and optimization for external block storage, this server is designed for memory-intensive database operations, Redis caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E64bs_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EBSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage but is engineered for high-performance Premium Block Storage. Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core CPU performance, high uncached memory bandwidth, and strong database and Redis throughput. Conversely, single-core metrics and large-scale LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, with the latter dropping to 2.76 tokens per second on a 70B model. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64bs_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It features strong multi-core performance and high block storage capability, but lacks local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64bs_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized, block-storage-capable virtual server featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs and strong multi-threaded performance for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB RAM based on Intel Xeon 8370C architecture.
Optimized for Premium Block Storage with no local storage included.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and uncached memory bandwidth.
High-throughput database operations and Redis caching capabilities.
Tradeoffs include average single-core speeds and slow large-model LLM text generation.
